# Test source code analysis for flag extraction

# First build the test CLI to have a binary
exec go build -o testcli testcli.go
! stderr .

# Create a simple CLI program with various flag types
exec cmd2mcpserver -dry-run -source . ./testcli
stdout '# Generated MCP server for testcli'
stdout '"format"'
stdout '"verbose"'
stdout '"count"'
stdout '"timeout"'

# Verify flag types are correctly identified
stdout '"type": "string".*format'
stdout '"type": "boolean".*verbose'
stdout '"type": "integer".*count'
stdout '"type": "number".*timeout'

# Verify descriptions are extracted
stdout '"description": "Output format"'
stdout '"description": "Enable verbose output"'
stdout '"description": "Number of iterations"'
stdout '"description": "Timeout in seconds"'

-- testcli.go --
package main

import (
	"flag"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	format := flag.String("format", "text", "Output format")
	verbose := flag.Bool("verbose", false, "Enable verbose output")
	count := flag.Int("count", 1, "Number of iterations")
	timeout := flag.Float64("timeout", 30.0, "Timeout in seconds")

	flag.Parse()

	fmt.Printf("Format: %s, Verbose: %v, Count: %d, Timeout: %f\n",
		*format, *verbose, *count, *timeout)
}
